title_zh: 黑色喜剧是为谁而拍的？
rubric_en: A new book is nostalgic for the ’90s. But the era of crossover success was not necessarily the pinnacle of Black comedic achievement.
rubric_zh: 一本新书对上世纪90年代充满了怀旧情绪，但“跨界成功”的时代未必是黑人喜剧艺术成就的顶峰。

paragraphs.en: Early in his career, Dave Chappelle worked on a sitcom concept with Fox. (Kwaku Alston / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 戴夫·查普尔在职业生涯初期曾与福克斯合作开发过一个情景喜剧构想。（Kwaku Alston / Getty）

paragraphs.en: Early in his career, before he became a household name, Dave Chappelle attempted to get a series off the ground at Fox. It was 1998, and the network had already welcomed Black comedies such as Living Single, Martin, and In Living Color—shows that implied a vested interest in Black taste. Chappelle might have expected to find a receptive audience when he presented the pilot for Dave Chappelle, a sitcom based on his life as a young Black comedian. What he reportedly got instead was a roomful of white executives with a familiar set of complaints. There were not enough white people on his proposed show. It was not “universal” enough—meaning it was too Black. Swap out the Black female lead for a white one and add an additional white character, he was advised. All of this despite Fox being, as Chappelle put it at the time, a network that had “built itself on black viewers.” He refused to cooperate, remarking that the incident “tells every black artist no matter what you do, you need whites to succeed.”
paragraphs.zh: 在他成名前的早期，戴夫·查普尔曾试图在福克斯推动一部连续剧的制作。那是1998年，福克斯已经播出了《单身生活》（《Living Single》）、《马丁》（《Martin》）和《喜剧天后之友》（《In Living Color》）等黑人喜剧，这些节目似乎显示出该网络对黑人口味有着既得利益。查普尔本可能预期，自己以年轻黑人喜剧演员生活为原型的情景喜剧《戴夫·查普尔》试播集会受到欢迎。可据说他得到的却是一屋子的白人高管和一套老套的抱怨：提案里白人不够多，不够“普适”——也就是说太“黑”了。有人建议把黑人女主换成白人，再加一个白人角色。更讽刺的是，尽管当时查普尔形容福克斯是一个“建立在黑人观众之上”的网络，他还是拒绝了合作，并评论说，这件事告诉所有黑人艺术家：不管你做什么，都必须依靠白人才能成功。

paragraphs.en: Chappelle was caught up in a whirlwind of change in the TV landscape. The successes of Black sitcoms in the 1980s and ’90s had made new shows marketable for mainstream (read: not just Black) audiences, but that marketability had come with a catch. In the late ’90s, there was a broader push for multiculturalism, which came to be defined, in a great many cases, as whiteness-plus: white protagonists with their Black, Asian, and Latino friends; Blackness not as a set of experiences in itself, but as proof positive of the white hero’s ability to learn. Jokes that might offend or even just confuse white audiences became harder sells, and safe crossover appeal became the norm. A few years prior to Chappelle’s pitch, Keenen Ivory Wayans, the creative force behind In Living Color, had left his show, over issues that reportedly included censorship from Fox.
paragraphs.zh: 查普尔正处在电视格局剧烈变动的漩涡之中。20世纪80年代和90年代黑人情景喜剧的成功，让新节目对主流（即不只是黑人）观众更具商业吸引力，但这种市场化也附带了代价。到90年代后期，社会更广泛地倡导“多元文化”，在许多情况下，这种“多元”被定义成“白色加”：白人主角加上其黑人、亚裔和拉美裔朋友；黑人性并未被当作一种独立经验，而成了证明白人英雄能否“学习成长”的正面教材。那些可能冒犯甚至只是让白人观众困惑的笑话，变得越来越难推销，而安全的“跨界吸引力”则成了常态。查普尔推介前几年，福克斯《喜剧天后之友》幕后核心人物基南·艾弗里·威恩斯（Keenen Ivory Wayans）已经离开了自己的节目，理由据称包括福克斯的审查。

paragraphs.en: From the October 2021 issue: Hannah Giorgis on the unwritten rules of Black TV
paragraphs.zh: 来自2021年10月号：Hannah Giorgis谈黑人电视的“未写明规则”

paragraphs.en: Fast-forward half a decade to the now-famous story of Chappelle’s Show, the sketch series that began in 2003 on Comedy Central. We live in the comedy world that Chappelle conquered, through the Miss Cleo parody, the racial draft, and “I’m Rick James, bitch!” He mastered a comic style that highlighted the absurdity of racism by taking it to its extremes, while also leaning on and exploring Black in-jokes without translating them for white viewers. His bits became memes before most of us knew what a meme was, and—taking advantage of Comedy Central’s comparatively lax rules—he insisted on using the N-word liberally.
paragraphs.zh: 把时间快进半个十年，就会来到如今广为人知的《查普尔秀》（Chappelle’s Show）故事：这部始于2003年在Comedy Central播出的脱口秀小品节目。今天的喜剧舞台上，我们生活在查普尔开辟出的风格里，他靠《迷丝·克里奥》（Miss Cleo）模仿、种族抽签（racial draft）段子和“我就是Rick James，bitch！”一举成名。他把嘲讽种族主义的滑稽方式推到极端，既精准揭示其荒诞性，也敢于依靠并深入挖掘黑人的内部梗，而不去给白人观众“翻译”这些梗。在大多数人还不知道“表情包”（meme）是什么意思之前，他的段子就已经先成为了表情包；并且他借助Comedy Central相对宽松的规定，公然大量使用了N词。

paragraphs.en: This Chappelle show was everything that the 1998 concept couldn’t be. And ironically, though the show made no effort whatsoever to be “universal,” it nevertheless won a rabid and diverse audience. In 2005, Chappelle walked away from a reported $50 million contract he’d signed with Comedy Central the previous year. Most people were baffled by his decision, but in interviews, he explained that the fame and pressure had weighed on him, and that the show’s popularity—especially among people who were ostensibly the butt of the jokes—indicated that his work had not been received as he’d intended. In the end, Chappelle followed the logic he set for himself early on in his career: Mainstream approval was never more important than the integrity of the material, or of its maker.
paragraphs.zh: 这档查普尔节目是1998年那种构想所无法做到的一切。讽刺的是，尽管节目完全没有“普适化”的打算，它却反而赢得了一个狂热而多元的观众群。2005年，查普尔退出了他前一年与Comedy Central签下的据称价值5000万美元的合约。大多数人都对他的选择感到困惑，但在采访中他解释说，名声和压力让他不堪重负，而且节目的人气——尤其是在那些表面上应该是笑料对象的人群中也很高——说明他的作品并没有按他最初预期的方式被接收。最终，查普尔坚持了自己职业生涯早期确立的原则：主流认可从未比作品本身，或者其创作者的完整性更重要。

paragraphs.en: Chappelle’s era of comedic dominance is the denouement of Black Out Loud: The Revolutionary History of Black Comedy From Vaudeville to ’90s Sitcoms, a new book by Geoff Bennett. A PBS News Hour anchor, Bennett traces the trajectory of Black comedy, beginning with the long backstory of its arrival in the network-TV mainstream. The first of the book’s two sections is a series of historical profiles of Black comics, from the minstrel-era performer Billy Kersands through Eddie Murphy. Bennett then turns to several landmark Black television shows of the ’80s and ’90s—series in which, as a youth, he could “see Blackness represented in a way that felt real and expansive.” The book concludes with Chappelle’s Show.
paragraphs.zh: 查普尔的喜剧统治时期构成了杰夫·贝内特（Geoff Bennett）新书《黑色大声吵醒：黑色喜剧从歌舞杂耍到90年代情景喜剧的革命史》（Black Out Loud: The Revolutionary History of Black Comedy From Vaudeville to ’90s Sitcoms）中的尾声。一位PBS《新闻一小时》（PBS News Hour）主播，贝内特追溯了黑色喜剧的发展轨迹，并从其进入有线电视主流、走向网络电视的漫长前史开始梳理。书的前半部分是对黑色喜剧人的历史画像串联，从小丑式早期舞台演员Billy Kersands一直写到Eddie Murphy。接着，贝内特转向80年代和90年代若干重要的黑人人气电视节目——这些节目让他在青年时期“得以看到黑人形象以一种真实且更广阔的方式被呈现”。本书以《查普尔秀》作结。

paragraphs.en: In Bennett’s resolutely progressive view, the story of Black comedy in America is of “a steady march toward fearlessness.” But Bennett measures progress mostly by the ability of Black comedy to break barriers and achieve crossover appeal. The effect is to render Black comedy that doesn’t go mainstream—that remains just “Black,” and thrives not in scripted shows on network television but on tours and in comedy clubs, specials, films, TikToks, and Instagram Reels—less relevant. This framing casts the grand lineage of Black comedy as a vehicle for racial reconciliation.
paragraphs.zh: 在贝内特坚定的进步叙事中，美国黑色喜剧的历史是一段“持续走向无所畏惧”的过程。但贝内特衡量进步，主要依据是黑色喜剧突破障碍并赢得跨界传播的能力。其效果是，把不走主流道路的黑色喜剧边缘化：那些始终保持“黑色”特质、主要在巡回演出、喜剧俱乐部、脱口秀特辑、电影、TikTok和Instagram Reels中蓬勃生长，而不在网络电视正剧里占据中心位置的喜剧，便被视作不够相关。这样的框架将黑色喜剧的宏大谱系描绘成了一个实现种族和解的工具。

paragraphs.en: As Bennett tells it, the struggle that persists down the “ancestral line” of Black comics is to courageously and cannily resist stereotypes, each generation less fettered than the one that came before it. That process begins with blackface, mammies, and Stepin Fetchits. Next come the winking subversions of those archetypes by the likes of Moms Mabley and Hattie McDaniel. They in turn lay the groundwork for the later, far more brash subversions of Redd Foxx, Dick Gregory, and in particular Richard Pryor, who perfected a singular brand of self-dissolving, linguistically nimble storytelling.
paragraphs.zh: 正如本内特所说，黑人喜剧一路传承下来的斗争，是要勇敢而精明地抵抗刻板印象，每一代人都比前一代人少些束缚。这个过程始于黑脸演绎、家庭女佣形象、以及“斯蒂平·费奇特”这类角色。随后，像莫姆斯·梅布莉和哈蒂·麦克丹尼这样喜剧人对这些原型进行了眨眼式的颠覆。她们/他她们又为后来更为大胆的颠覆者奠定基础，包括雷德·福克斯、迪克·格雷戈里，尤其是理查德·普赖尔——他完善了一种独特的、自我解构式、语言上灵活机敏的叙事喜剧风格。

paragraphs.en: Richard Pryor’s controversial stand-up routines made way for a new generation of comics. (Fotos International / Columbia Pictures / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 理查德·普赖尔颇具争议的单口喜剧节目为新一代喜剧人让路。（Fotos International / Columbia Pictures / Getty）

paragraphs.en: Black Out Loud is an account of successive generations of Black comics gradually persuading white audiences to see beyond caricature and accept Black characters as people whose stories are worth paying attention to. In the ’90s era of peak Black sitcom, Bennett sees, on the one hand, a reflection of America’s begrudging acceptance of Black culture, and on the other, the realization of Black viewers’ demand for sitcoms that offered authentic representation. Sitcoms made for Black audiences prior to the 1990s were rarely under Black creative control, and even 1970s-era classics such as Good Times and The Jeffersons offered up imperfect or stereotypical simulacra of Black life. With more Black creative input shaping successors such as The Cosby Show; its spin-off, A Different World, set at a historically Black college; and The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air, however, the stories subtly poked at the rhythms that had been familiar to Black TV. For example, when, on The Fresh Prince, Will and Carlton are detained by racist cops while driving a Mercedes, the officers’ presumption that the car must be stolen doubles as commentary on the show’s popularity and on Black upward mobility generally: We’re not supposed to be here.
paragraphs.zh: 《黑色大笑》记录了几代黑人喜剧人如何逐步说服白人观众走出讽刺夸张的印象，把黑人角色看作值得关注的人类故事主体。谈到九十年代黑人情景喜剧的黄金时期，本内特看到的是，一方面美国对黑人文化的勉强接纳，另一方面也看到了黑人观众对提供真实再现的情景喜剧的需求。九十年代以前为黑人观众制作的喜剧节目很少由黑人创作者主导，甚至像《善良时代》和《杰斐逊家族》这样的1970年代经典，也多是对黑人生活的并不完美、甚至带有刻板印象的模拟。但在后续作品中，随着《新科比秀》、其衍生剧《另一个世界》（设定在一所历史悠久的黑人大学）和《贝尔艾尔的漂亮男孩》都受到更多黑人创作力量影响，这些故事开始微妙地戳破黑电视里早已熟悉的节奏。例如，在《贝尔艾尔的漂亮男孩》中，威尔和卡尔顿在开一辆梅赛德斯时遭到种族主义警察盘查，警察认定那辆车必定是被盗，这既是对该剧受欢迎程度的评论，也是在评论黑人阶层上升流动的一般现象：我们不应该出现在这里。

paragraphs.en: It’s a little strange, though, to lurch, as the book does, from the bawdy confrontations of Pryor—who tested his audience’s comfort with brash profanity and jokes about racial violence and graphic sex, including gay sex—to the wholesome fare of Family Matters, in which someone spray-painting the N-word on Laura Winslow’s locker and telling her to go back to Africa was the subject of a Very Special Episode. Strange, that is, to fashion the era of Urkel as proof of a clear advancement from the era of Pryor. Progressive views of 20th-century America are right, in many ways, to give us a sense of the cultural politics that were pushing the country forward: more rights, more opportunity, more representation. But the approach stumbles when it too eagerly smooths over the equally characteristic setbacks, divots, and reversals.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，从本书中从普赖尔那种赤裸直白的冲撞——他用强烈粗口和关于种族暴力与露骨性行为（包括同性性行为）的笑料测试观众的心理承受力——转向《家有儿女》那种健康向上的喜剧内容，确实有点别扭。在《家有儿女》中，甚至有人在劳拉·温斯洛的储物柜上涂喷漆写下 N 字头词并叫她“回到非洲”，这居然成为一集“非常特别剧情”要处理的主题。也就是说，把乌克尔时代当作明显优于普赖尔时代的进步证据，确实很奇怪。二十世纪美国进步观念在很多方面确实能让我们感受到推动社会前行的文化政治：更多权利、更多机会、更多代表性。但这种观念会失误在于，它过于急于抹平同样具有时代特征的挫折、沟壑与逆转。

paragraphs.en: Family Matters was one of the longest-running sitcoms with a mostly Black cast. (ABC / Everett Collection)
paragraphs.zh: 《家庭事务》是播出时间最长、以黑人演员为主的情景喜剧之一。（美国广播公司/埃弗里特文献馆）

paragraphs.en: Still, Bennett is compelling in his insistence that what unites the various threads of Black comedic genius is an overarching trend toward Black comics getting to be themselves. His reporting on Wayans’s self-described “dictatorship” as In Living Color’s creator, for example, highlights how, even at the risk of seeming irreverent or disrespectful, Wayans tried to fend off network meddling in achieving his singular vision. That doggedness helped chart a path for many of Bennett’s subjects, including the rest of the Wayanses. This book is a genealogy, yes, but also a relay, stretching from performers hiding—literally—beneath layers of burnt cork to, a century later, Chappelle donning a blond wig and beige makeup to play a delectably too-white news anchor who’s even funnier for looking not quite right (or white).
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，贝内特的观点仍很有说服力，因为他坚持认为，将黑人喜剧天才的不同脉络连在一起的共同趋势，是黑人喜剧人越来越能够做真实的自己。以他对韦恩斯自称“专断”——即《明星大道》创作者——的报道为例，便可看出，即便有冒犯轻蔑或不敬的风险，韦恩斯也努力抵御电视网干预，以实现他独特的创作愿景。正是这种执拗，给贝内特笔下的许多人物开辟了道路，也包括其他韦恩斯家族成员。这本书确实是一部“家谱”，但同时也是一场接力：从演员们在焦黑软木上面具下方（字面意义上的“隐藏”）表演，到一个世纪后，查普尔戴着金发假发和米色舞台妆，饰演一位“刚刚好”不太合适——甚至不算白——的新闻主播角色，反而更具喜剧效果。

paragraphs.en: Read: Geoff Bennett on the In Living Color effect
paragraphs.zh: 阅读：Geoff Bennett 讲《In Living Color》的影响

paragraphs.en: The satire cannot exist without the real. The history of blackface is what makes the cutting humor of Chappelle’s whiteface possible. In that respect, Bennett’s vision of Black comedy’s evolution as “a steady march toward fearlessness” makes undeniable sense. How did we get from Bert Williams and George Walker—two extraordinary comic talents whose artistic legacy is complicated by their association with blackface—to Black comedians being afforded the leverage to turn that shameful history into an epic in-joke? Part of the answer is the work of people like Hattie McDaniel, who is said to have quipped that she’d rather play a maid than be one. Bennett’s effort to track this history incrementally, through heroes whose symbolic cultural wins had measurable impact on what came after, makes for a buoyant and agreeable book.
paragraphs.zh: 讽刺不能脱离现实而存在。正是黑脸历史，使查普尔的“白脸”犀利幽默成为可能。从这个意义上说，贝内特将黑人喜剧的演进视为“一条不断走向无畏的稳步进军”是完全说得通的。我们是如何从伯特·威廉姆斯（Bert Williams）和乔治·沃克（George Walker）走到今天的？这两位杰出的喜剧天才在艺术成就之外，又因与黑脸表演相关而留下了复杂的历史烙印。如今黑人喜剧人被赋予了把这段羞耻历史改写为一出史诗级“圈内笑话”的影响力，这一变化是如何发生的？答案的一部分在于像哈蒂·麦克丹尼尔（Hattie McDaniel）这样的人。她据说曾打趣说，自己宁愿扮演女仆也不愿成为女仆。贝内特通过一系列象征性文化胜利的“先行者”来逐步梳理这段历史，并追踪这些胜利对后续发展所产生的实际影响，因此写成了一部轻快且颇有说服力的好书。

paragraphs.en: Where he errs, however, is in holding up the era of crossover appeal as the apex of Black comic achievement. To dwell on ’90s network sitcoms—on Black television playing on white networks in general—is to gauge the progress of Black comedy in terms that are not always those of Black comedians themselves. We can see the success of shows such as In Living Color and Family Matters as proof that the American experiment, with its promises of integration and acceptance, has finally worked. Or we can acknowledge that the promise has always been contingent: You can tell jokes about race, but …
paragraphs.zh: 然而，他的失误在于把“跨界吸引力”的时代当作黑人喜剧成就的巅峰。把目光停留在90年代的收视喜剧上——泛指黑人电视剧在白人电视网播出——就会用一种并不总是黑人喜剧演员本人认可的标准，去衡量黑人喜剧的发展。我们可以把《有生之年》（《In Living Color》）和《Family Matters》等节目的成功解读为美国实验的一种验证：它曾许下的融合与接纳承诺终于实现了。或者，我们也可以承认这种承诺始终是有条件的：你可以讲种族笑话，但……

paragraphs.en: Late in his life, the great Dick Gregory argued that Black people should never turn to entertainment expecting liberation, and that white comedians were not burdened by these unrealistic hopes. “Bob Hope ain’t never changed nothing,” he said. “Fred Astaire ain’t never fixed it so little children could eat.” But, as Black Out Loud makes clear, Black popular entertainers are charged with needing to stand for something: Liberation by way of representation has long been a demand. Chappelle put it plainly. “White people, white artists, are allowed to be individuals,” he told Esquire in 2006, defending his choice to leave his show. “But we always have this greater struggle that we at least have to keep in mind somewhere.”
paragraphs.zh: 在生命晚期，伟大的迪克·格雷戈里（Dick Gregory）曾主张黑人不该把希望寄托在娱乐中以求解放，也指出白人喜剧人并不背负这些不切实际的期待。他说：“鲍勃·霍普从来没有改变过任何事。弗雷德·阿斯泰尔也没有把自己改得让一些孩子能吃上饭。”但《黑人怒吼》（Black Out Loud）清楚地表明，黑人流行艺人被要求承担一种使命：长期以来，作为代表发出“通过可见性实现解放”的诉求一直存在。查普尔说得很直接。他在2006年对《Esquire》说：“白人、白人艺术家，被允许成为个人。”他当时是在为自己退出节目辩护。“但我们始终有一场更大的斗争，至少在某个地方，必须始终铭记。”

paragraphs.en: Of course, Chappelle did return after his hiatus, and the trajectory of his comeback is one of quite a few inconvenient truths that Bennett’s book, interesting as it is, cannot account for. His relentlessly progressive arc struggles to explain the present: when Chappelle is controversial for jokes not about white people but about trans people and Jews; when networks seem to have short leashes for the few Black shows they do green-light; when audiences are atomized and moving away from television altogether, and toward comedians who make their bones on self-produced short-form videos; and when jokes about race land differently, given an openly white-supremacist administration in the White House.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，查普尔在中断之后确实复出了，而他的复出轨迹中有许多棘手现实，是贝内特（Bennett）的书尽管有趣，却无法解释的。书中一条不懈向前的叙事线在解释当下时会显得吃力：当查普尔因笑点不再只是针对白人，而是针对跨性别者和犹太人而引发争议；当电视网对寥寥几个他们批准播出的黑人节目都“牵一发而动全身”；当观众被分割，整体脱离了电视，转向了靠自制短视频走红的喜剧演员；以及当有关种族的笑话在白宫拥有公开白人至上主义政权的背景下，所传达的意义发生了变化。

paragraphs.en: Read: Does Dave Chappelle find anything funnier than being canceled?
paragraphs.zh: 阅读：戴夫·查普尔找到了比“被封杀”更有趣的东西吗？

paragraphs.en: Bennett scopes his inquiry to focus on the form that most interests him—and, likely, that makes for a manageable book—but the choice constrains his vision of fearlessness to one that mostly involved speaking to white audiences in a decade of multicultural awareness, and through a network-dominated medium that would soon be on its way out. What if fearlessness, though, has nothing to do with white audiences or gatekeepers? If Chappelle himself represents the pinnacle of Black comedic courage, then maybe his most important contribution to the canon isn’t the Netflix specials or the Prince sketch, but the famous moment when he turned to a camera—and implicitly to Black viewers—and cried: “This racism is killing me inside!”
paragraphs.zh: 本特恩将研究范围限定在他最感兴趣的那种形式上——也可能正因如此才让这本书更容易完成——但这种选择把他对“无所畏惧”的想象，限制在这样一种形态：在一个多元文化意识高度敏感的年代里，主要向白人观众发声，并借助一种即将走向没落、由电视网络主导的媒介。如果“无所畏惧”与白人观众或把关者毫无关系呢？如果查普尔本人正是黑人喜剧勇气的巅峰象征，那么他对漫画史最重要的贡献，也许不是《网飞》脱口秀专场或《皇帝》小品，而是那一刻他转向镜头，也就是转向黑人人群，含蓄地对他们说出：“这种种族主义正在从内心摧毁我！”

paragraphs.en: Whether or not you subscribe to the idea that a Black artist’s job is to change the world, a history of that responsibility that sticks to the mainstream hardly paints a full picture. The real legacy of revolutionary Black comedy might lead us not just to Family Matters and Chappelle’s Show, but also to the terrain claimed by Black comedy that primarily serves Black audiences—the way things were for the likes of Gregory and Flip Wilson, and for comedians such as the late Paul Mooney and Patrice O’Neal and the still-kicking, still-hilarious Katt Williams.
paragraphs.zh: 无论你是否认同“黑人艺术家的职责是改变世界”这一观念，单单依赖紧贴主流文化的这套责任叙事，都无法勾勒出完整图景。革命性黑人喜剧的真正遗产，或许会把我们引向的不仅是《家庭纷争》与查普尔秀，也会引向黑人喜剧主要为黑人观众服务的那片天地——想想格雷戈里与弗利普·威尔逊这样的先行者，以及已故的保罗·穆尼、帕特里斯·奥尼尔，和至今仍在场仍然令人捧腹的卡特·威廉姆斯等喜剧演员。

paragraphs.en: As much as I’ve missed Chappelle’s Show since it went kaput, I’ve missed shows such as Def Comedy Jam and BET’s Comic View—the latter of which launched the careers of D. L. Hughley and Cedric the Entertainer—even more. These shows produced a generation of comics, actors, and presenters who are famous among Black audiences but relatively unknown in white pop culture. Wary as I am of calling the often-aggravating work of Tyler Perry “revolutionary,” the bulk of it, by and large, still appeals to Black audiences. And only to Black audiences. The longest-lived Black sitcom, by episode count, is not The Jeffersons, The Cosby Show, or Family Matters, but Tyler Perry’s House of Payne, which aired its 400th show this year. The aspirations of Payne and its ilk are not to cross over, nor to reify the values of the Black middle class, but simply to be funny. And Black. All on their own terms. What’s a revolutionary history without that?
paragraphs.zh: 我并不比别人更少想念查普尔秀的停播，但我更想念《嘲笑之夜》（Def Comedy Jam）和 BET 的《Comic View》——后者催生了 D. L. Hughley 与 Cedric the Entertainer 的事业——。这些节目造就了一代喜剧演员、影视演员和主持人，他们在黑人观众中广为人知，却在白人流行文化里相对鲜为人知。尽管我对泰勒·佩里的许多作品常常令人不快却仍称不上“革命性”，但从整体上看，它们依旧主要吸引黑人观众，而且只吸引黑人观众。按集数来算，寿命最长的黑人情景喜剧并不是《杰弗逊一家》《考比秀》或《家庭问题》，而是泰勒·佩里的《佩恩之家》，今年已播出第400集。《佩恩一家》及其同类作品的目标并不是“跨界”，也不是重塑黑人中产阶级的价值观，而只是要把人逗笑。并且是黑人式的笑。完全按自己的逻辑、自己的方式。没有那样的革命历史又算什么？

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the May 2026 print edition with the headline “Who Is Black Comedy For?”
paragraphs.zh: 本文刊登在2026年5月印刷版的标题是“黑人喜剧是写给谁看的？”
